description_long: This object is a pair of standard pliers, featuring two elongated, slightly curved handles that converge at a central pivot joint. The jaws are robust and flat with a slight taper, designed for gripping, bending, or cutting wires and other materials. The handles appear to be made of metal, possibly steel, with a dark finish and a subtle, smooth texture, lacking any visible rubber or plastic insulation. The pivot mechanism is a classic fixed joint, allowing the jaws to open and close symmetrically. There is a small, rectangular marking on one handle, but no clear brand or logo is visible. The overall construction suggests durability and utility for general-purpose tasks in urban environments such as garages, workshops, or street maintenance.
description: A sturdy pair of metal pliers with flat, tapered jaws and long, slightly curved handles, finished in a dark metallic color. The design is simple and utilitarian, suitable for gripping or cutting tasks.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the pliers, with both handles and the flat, tapered jaws clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the thin profile of the pliers, highlighting the narrowness of the jaws and handles.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, showing the handles and jaws from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profile and the alignment of the handles and jaws.
attribute_handle_type: Long, straight metal handles with a slight outward curve, no visible insulation or ergonomic features.
attribute_jaw_design: Flat, tapered jaws with a robust gripping surface, lacking serrations or wire-cutting notches; standard for general-purpose pliers.
attribute_pivot_mechanism: Fixed central pivot joint, non-adjustable, allowing symmetrical opening and closing of the jaws.
attribute_grip_pattern: Smooth metal surface with no visible ribbing, knurling, or rubberized grip.
